title: Fitness and wellbeing instructors
description: Teach or instruct individuals or groups for the primary purpose of self-enrichment or recreation, rather than for an occupational objective, educational attainment, competition, or fitness. Instruct or coach groups or individuals in the fundamentals of sports for the primary purpose of competition. Demonstrate techniques and methods of participation. May evaluate athletes' strengths and weaknesses as possible recruits or to improve the athletes' technique to prepare them for competition. Those required to hold teaching certifications should be reported in the appropriate teaching category. Officiate at competitive athletic or sporting events. Detect infractions of rules and decide penalties according to established regulations. Includes all sporting officials, referees, and competition judges. (Based on 8 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Plan, organize, and conduct practice sessions and sports-related activities, such as training camps and skill improvement courses.
Teach and explain the rules and regulations governing a specific sport, and instruct individuals or groups in sports rules, game strategies, and performance principles.
Evaluate athletes' skills and review performance records to determine their fitness and potential, and assess and report the progress of recovering athletes.
Confer with other sporting officials, coaches, players, and facility managers to provide information, coordinate activities, and address problems.
Monitor athletes' use of equipment and ensure they adhere to safety regulations during practices and competitions.
Officiate at sporting events or competitions to maintain standards of play and ensure that game rules are observed.
Provide training direction, encouragement, motivation, and nutritional advice to prepare athletes for competitions.
Evaluate individuals' abilities, needs, and physical conditions, and develop suitable training programs to meet their requirements.
Conduct an initial assessment of an athlete's injury or illness to provide emergency or continued care, and apply protective devices to reduce injury risk.
Plan and implement comprehensive athletic injury or illness prevention programs, and monitor participants' progress and adapt programs as needed.
Teach proper exercise techniques, breathing techniques, and ways to maintain exertion levels to maximize benefits from exercise routines.
Inspect sporting equipment and facilities for compliance with safety regulations, and ensure participant eligibility for events.
Report to regulating organizations regarding sporting activities, complaints, and necessary actions, such as penalties or disciplinary actions.
Analyze the strengths and weaknesses of opposing teams to develop strategies for upcoming games and competitions.
Resolve claims of rule infractions or complaints by participants and assess any necessary penalties according to established regulations.
Observe participants and inform them of corrective measures necessary for skill improvement, adapting teaching methods to meet varying needs.
Administer first aid for injuries and provide resources regarding nutrition, weight control, and lifestyle issues.
Organize and conduct competitions, tournaments, and events to promote sport participation and community engagement.
Train individuals or groups in proper techniques and methods for fitness and wellbeing activities.
Evaluate participants' performance to ensure suitability for fitness activities and improve their techniques.
Instruct or coach clients in the fundamentals of sports for recreation and self-enrichment.
Meet with team members or other instructors to discuss program changes or participant needs.
Requisition necessary supplies and equipment to support fitness programs.
Participate in continuing education to stay current with trends in fitness and wellbeing.
Apply customer feedback and suggestions to improve fitness instruction or services.
